item.text: I’ll locate the `agent_eval` module and its existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block first, then add the requested test and run the relevant crate tests.
I found the target in `context-harness/src/agent_eval.rs`; next I’m inspecting the existing classification tests to add the new case in the same style.
I confirmed `classify_result` short-circuits on `valid_for_comparison`; I’m adding a focused unit test that exercises multiple conflicting score combinations and verifies it still returns `Excluded`.
The test is in place; next I’ll run formatting and then the targeted test suite for the `context-harness` crate to verify it passes.

Added the requested unit test `classify_result_excluded_when_pair_invalid` in [context-harness/src/agent_eval.rs:2000](/private/var/folders/bn/jq675y6x5m1d1x83vyh99f5c0000gn/T/codex-arm-XXXXXX.ui7xjNuFw3/codex-rs/context-harness/src/agent_eval.rs:2000).

What it asserts:
- `classify_result(..., valid_for_comparison = false, ...)` returns `AgentEvalResult::Excluded` even when scores favor either side.
- It checks both:
  - explicit `excluded_reason` passthrough
  - `None` fallback to `"invalid"`.

Validation run:
- `just fmt` (required formatting pass)
- `just test -p codex-context-harness` (fails in existing timeout-heavy `ri_packet_regressions` tests)
- `just test -p codex-context-harness classify_result_excluded_when_pair_invalid` (passes)
